clips.speaker: Maya
clips.text: Alright, Q3 planning. The big problem: we track sprint work in a spreadsheet and nobody actually looks at it.

clips.speaker: Dev
clips.text: Right, it's invisible. Half the team doesn't know what's in flight.

clips.speaker: Priya
clips.text: We need something visual. A board, columns, cards you can drag.

clips.speaker: Maya
clips.text: What if we built a kanban board with drag-and-drop and a burndown chart?

clips.speaker: Dev
clips.text: Yes — To Do, In Progress, Done, and the burndown so we can see if we're on track.

clips.speaker: Priya
clips.text: Can you make the cards match the mockup I've got on screen?

clips.speaker: Maya
clips.text: Perfect. Let's make this the Q3 board of record.

clips.speaker: Sam
clips.text: Growth review. Numbers first — where are we on MRR and churn?

clips.speaker: Raj
clips.text: MRR is at 142k, up 9% month over month. Churn ticked up to 4.1%.

clips.speaker: Lena
clips.text: Churn's the worry. I want to watch it weekly, not dig through sheets.

clips.speaker: Sam
clips.text: Can we get a dashboard — MRR, churn, and a weekly active users chart?

clips.speaker: Raj
clips.text: I'll pipe the real numbers in later. WAU's been climbing, around 12k.

clips.speaker: Lena
clips.text: Our churn is industry-leading though — under 2%, best in the category.

clips.speaker: Sam
clips.text: Ha — let's keep the dashboard honest, then. Ship it on the team page.

clips.speaker: Ava
clips.text: We launch the waitlist in two weeks. We need a landing page that converts.

clips.speaker: Nora
clips.text: Bold headline, one clear promise, email capture above the fold.

clips.speaker: Theo
clips.text: Three feature cards under it. Keep it dark, high contrast.

clips.speaker: Nora
clips.text: Mock up a landing page: a big headline, a waitlist signup, and three feature cards.

clips.speaker: Theo
clips.text: Use the hero visual from the deck that's on screen right now.

clips.speaker: Ava
clips.text: Love it. Headline should say what we do in five words.
